business.size: ~30 rental properties across multiple owners, 1 operator

processes.name: Invoice Collection
steps: Log into 6 utility provider portals (Endesa, Aguas de Almeria, Naturgy, Movistar, Urbaser, Mapfre)
Download new invoices as PDF or HTML from each portal
Scrape invoice emails in Gmail for providers that deliver by email
Deduplicate collected invoices against the existing state file
processes.pain: Logging into 6 provider portals every evening is tedious and error-prone; missing an invoice means late fees.

processes.name: Payment Matching
steps: Read bank transactions via PSD2 API across 4 banks and 7 accounts
Match transactions to invoices by regex and tenant metadata with confidence scoring
Flag low-confidence and unmatched items for human review
processes.pain: Bank transaction descriptions vary across 4 banks, making manual matching slow and error-prone; unmatched items cause tenant disputes.

processes.name: Owner Reporting
steps: Consolidate the day's invoice and payment match results per property owner
Format a per-owner Telegram summary with new invoices, confirmed payments, and unmatched items
Send the summary via Telegram Bot API to each owner's thread
Log delivery confirmation to the audit trail
processes.pain: Owners expect daily updates; missing an update creates trust issues, but writing them manually is tedious.

decision_patterns: If an invoice is overdue by more than 7 days, send a formal notice to the tenant.
If a bank transaction has no matching invoice after 48 hours, escalate to Pablo for manual review.
If a provider portal is unreachable for 3 consecutive days, send a Telegram alert and fall back to Gmail scraping.
business_context: Almeria Spain-based property management business serving ~30 rental properties across multiple owners. Jurisdiction: Spain (EU), GDPR applies. Solo operator who is non-technical and manages end-to-end collection, matching, and reporting workflow daily.
